mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and short-term earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of observations during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in >65% of triggered instances across tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained economic expansion and wage-driven consumption, supporting corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 60-day returns in >50% of monthly observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirms a technical recession, forcing equity market repricing and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of confirmed recession quarters during historical late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total payrolls signal synchronized supply and labor market strength, driving short-term risk-on sentiment and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of monthly observations during recovery regimes.
